body.title: cc_queue.py cmd_claim must check worker — placebo freeze allowed Igor to grab claude-worker tickets
body.result: pe_chain HYPOTHESIZE produced verbatim-valid source edit (cmd_claim now checks worker — placebo freeze becomes real). Test added by CC to fulfill multi-edit gap. Cert walk-02 substitute: PARTIAL (engram-level success on source edit, CC added test = doesn't count toward 10-without-help). Commit b092865d.

metadata.cert_substitute_reason: original walk-02 underlying had no real work left (already shipped via T-consult-confidence-gate)

body.description:
cmd_claim() in lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py only checks `status == 'pending'`. It does not honor the `worker` field, so cert_worker_freeze.py (which flips worker=igor → worker=claude as a kill-switch) is a placebo: pe_chain still successfully claims any pending ticket regardless of worker. Cert walk-02 caught this — Igor empty-closed T-consult-confidence-threshold-raise overnight despite being 'frozen' as worker=claude.

Fix: cmd_claim should reject the claim when worker is set and not 'igor'. The pe_chain caller is Igor; tickets explicitly tagged for claude (or other workers) should not be auto-claimable by Igor.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (cmd_claim function around line 270), tests/test_cc_queue.py or new tests/test_cc_queue_claim.py
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — cmd_claim worker check + matching test. OUT — cmd_done/cmd_block worker checks (separate ticket if needed); changing the worker semantics elsewhere.
**Test plan:** new test asserts cmd_claim rejects worker=claude tickets and accepts worker=igor or worker=None. Existing 4217 tests still pass.
